steps.title: Recognize as difference of squares
steps.content: The equation is $x^2 - \frac{25}{81} = 0$. Recognize that $\frac{25}{81} = \left(\frac{5}{9}\right)^2$, so we can rewrite as: $$x^2 - \left(\frac{5}{9}\right)^2 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and identify solutions
steps.content: Apply the difference of squares formula: $$\left(x - \frac{5}{9}\right)\left(x + \frac{5}{9}\right) = 0$$ Each factor equals zero when $x = \frac{5}{9}$ or $x = -\frac{5}{9}$. These are two distinct real solutions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
